questions.question: What is the primary difference between the set of Natural Numbers (ℕ) and the set of Whole Numbers (ℙ)?
options: Whole numbers include the element zero (0).
Natural numbers include decimals and fractions.
Both sets are identical in all contexts.
questions.explanation: Whole numbers are defined as the union of natural numbers and the element zero: ℙ = ℕ ∪ {0}.

questions.question: Which element acts as the additive identity in the set of Whole Numbers?
questions.explanation: An additive identity element leaves any number unchanged when added. Since a + 0 = a for any whole number a, 0 is the additive identity.

questions.question: Which whole number lacks a predecessor within the set of Whole Numbers (ℙ)?
questions.explanation: The predecessor of a number n is n - 1. For n = 0, the predecessor is -1, which is not a member of the set ℙ. Hence, 0 has no predecessor in ℙ.
